bead.title: website: audit bundled CLI docs for retired ddx agent namespace
bead.description:
PROBLEM
The parent Fizeau-boundary bead's search scope includes generated/bundled website docs under `.agents/skills/website` and `.claude/skills/website`. These pages still advertise retired `ddx agent` commands, quorum flags, and `ddx work` as an alias for `ddx agent execute-loop`, which conflicts with the no-compatibility-alias direction.

CURRENT STATE WITH FILE:LINE
- .agents/skills/website/content/docs/cli/_index.md:53-57 still lists `ddx agent run`, quorum usage, list, usage, and capabilities examples.
- .agents/skills/website/content/docs/cli/commands/ddx_agent.md:6-77 still documents a `ddx agent` command tree and examples.
- .agents/skills/website/content/docs/cli/commands/ddx_work.md:13-21 describes `ddx work` as an alias for `ddx agent execute-loop` and tells readers to reach for execute-loop.
- .claude/skills/website/content/docs/skills.md:15-84 still documents a `/ddx-agent` guided dispatch skill and `ddx agent run --quorum` examples.

PROPOSED FIX
- Update generated/bundled website docs in both `.agents/skills/website` and `.claude/skills/website` so public workflow examples use `ddx run`, `ddx try`, and `ddx work` directly.
- Remove or clearly retire pages that present `ddx agent` as an active workflow namespace or compatibility alias.
- Preserve legitimate external-agent terminology only where it describes AI agents or historical docs with explicit retired-context wording.
- If these pages are generated from another source, update the source and regenerate the copies rather than patching only one output copy.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change production CLI behavior in this bead.
- Do not edit core FEAT/ADR docs; sibling children own those surfaces.
- Do not remove examples of `--harness`, `--provider`, or `--model` when they are explicitly described as raw passthrough constraints.

PARENT + DEPS
Parent: ddx-424fdac4. No deps; this is one decomposition child of the broad Fizeau-boundary ratification bead.
bead.acceptance:
1. Website docs in `.agents/skills/website` and `.claude/skills/website` no longer present `ddx agent` as an active workflow namespace, no longer describe `ddx work` as an alias for `ddx agent execute-loop`, and no longer show quorum flags as core CLI behavior.
2. Public workflow examples use `ddx run`, `ddx try`, `ddx work`, and skill workflows such as compare-prompts/adversarial-review where appropriate.
3. `rg -n "ddx agent|ddx-agent|execute-loop|quorum|compatibility alias|alias for" .agents/skills/website .claude/skills/website` returns only audited historical/retired-context hits or literal external-agent references.
4. If website docs are generated, the source and generated copies are updated together; if they are not generated in this repo, the bead notes state that and identify the authoritative source checked.
5. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... -run "TestDDxSkillCopiedTreesMatchSource|TestInitRegistersSkills" -count=1` passes.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.

events.body: {"rationale":"Well-structured bead with clear file:line citations, numbered ACs, proper phase/area/kind/spec labels, explicit parent and no-deps statement, and a standalone description that contains no /tmp or chat references. Three issues reduce confidence in unattended execution: (1) AC 5 gates on specific Go test functions (TestDDxSkillCopiedTreesMatchSource, TestInitRegistersSkills) that may not exist — this is a docs-only bead with no stated Go code changes, so an agent cannot know whether those tests are present and runnable; (2) the label spec:FEAT-006 appears with no corresponding mention in the description or acceptance criteria, leaving an agent unable to verify its relevance; (3) AC 4 contains a conditional prose obligation ('the bead notes state that and identify the authoritative source checked') that is ambiguous about what artifact the agent must produce and where.","score":7,"suggested_fixes":["Verify before dispatch that TestDDxSkillCopiedTreesMatchSource and TestInitRegistersSkills exist in cli/cmd/...; if they do not, either add an AC that creates them or replace AC 5 with a test filter that is known to pass on a docs-only change (e.g., the broader './cmd/...' without a -run filter, or omit -run entirely).","Add one sentence to the description explaining what FEAT-006 governs and how it relates to this bead's scope, or remove the spec:FEAT-006 label if the connection is incidental.","Rewrite AC 4 to name a concrete deliverable: e.g., 'If docs are auto-generated, a comment in the PR body identifies the authoritative source file and confirms both source and generated copy were updated; if not generated, no additional artifact is required.'"],"waivers_applied":[]}
